decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Since geometry changes are hitting legality limits and recent improvements have stalled, the strategy shifts to material tuning. I will increase the heat spreader conductivity by a factor of 1.5 (from 425.25 to ~637.88), which is within the allowed [0.5x, 2.0x] step ratio and below the upper bound of 500.0 W/(m*K) is not possible, so I must cap at the bound or check the bound again. Wait, the bound is 500.0. The current value is 425.25. A 2.0x multiplier would be 850.5, which violates the hard bound of 500.0. Therefore, I will set the conductivity to the maximum allowable value of 500.0 to maximize lateral heat spreading without violating parameter bounds.

changes.reason: Maximizing spreader conductivity within the defined bound (500.0) to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce the chip peak temperature, as geometry adjustments are currently constrained.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature from 89.26°C towards the 85.0°C constraint
Improved thermal uniformity across the heat spreader layer
risk_notes: Setting conductivity to the upper bound may yield diminishing returns if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions
If this single change does not satisfy the constraint, subsequent steps may require increasing base_material conductivity or adjusting heat source scenarios
